questions.text: What is the main advantage of using Specs before implementing a feature?

options.label: Ensures alignment between requirements, design, and implementation before writing code
Allows skipping the design phase since requirements contain all technical details
Eliminates the need for automated tests because the Spec already validates all behavior
Guarantees that the implementation won't need code review

questions.explanation: Using Specs before implementing ensures that requirements, design, and tasks are aligned, reducing rework and guaranteeing that implementation meets actual needs.

questions.text: In a real workflow with Kiro, it is recommended to execute all tasks at once without intermediate verification.
questions.explanation: The recommended workflow is to execute tasks incrementally with verification after each step, ensuring that errors are detected early and don't propagate to subsequent tasks.

questions.text: Arrange the complete bugfix workflow with Kiro in the correct order:

options.label: Investigate and identify the bug's root cause
Write a test that reproduces the bug
Verify the test passes with the fix

questions.explanation: The bugfix workflow with Kiro follows: investigate the root cause, write an exploration test that confirms the bug, implement the fix, and verify that the test now passes.
